seoTitle: Embed a Microsoft Forms Poll or Survey in Outlook
metaDescription: Insert an interactive poll or survey link directly into an Outlook email using the built-in Forms integration, no separate browser tab required.
intro: Dropping a raw survey link into an email often means low engagement, since recipients have to leave their inbox to respond. Outlook's built-in Forms integration lets you embed a quick poll directly into the message body or attach an email-optimized survey link that feels native to the email itself.

steps.heading: Start a new email
steps.body: Open Outlook and click New Mail to begin a fresh draft.

steps.heading: Insert an inline poll
steps.body: Place your cursor in the message body, click the Insert tab, and select the Poll icon to open the Forms sidebar.

steps.heading: Build your quick question
steps.body: Enter your question text and answer choices, toggle Multiple Answers if needed, then click Next.

steps.heading: Add it to your email
steps.body: Click Add to email to drop the interactive voting card directly into your draft.

steps.heading: Or link a full survey
steps.body: For a longer survey, open the Forms portal, click Collect Responses, and select the Email shortcut to generate a link optimized for email distribution before sending.

faq.question: Can recipients vote without leaving their inbox?
faq.answer: Yes, inline polls added through the Insert tab let recipients vote directly inside the email itself.

faq.question: What's the difference between an inline poll and an embedded survey link?
faq.answer: An inline poll is a quick multiple-choice question built directly in Outlook, while an embedded link points to a full Forms survey.

faq.question: Can I keep poll responses anonymous?
faq.answer: Anonymity depends on your Forms and organizational settings — review your response tracking options before sending.

faq.question: Will the poll display correctly on mobile email clients?
faq.answer: Yes, but it's worth testing your message across devices to confirm buttons and text render properly.
